data.acceptance:
1. Each fix operation prints a status line before it begins, e.g. `Fixing <issue>...`.
2. First output appears within 1 second of the command starting (no silent hang).
3. A summary line is printed on completion, e.g. `All fixes applied.` or `N issue(s) fixed.`.
4. `kno doctor` (without `--fix`) produces no new output — existing behavior is unchanged.
5. If there are no issues to fix, the command prints an appropriate message (e.g. `No issues found.`) and exits cleanly.
6. Verified by running `kno doctor --fix` in a terminal and observing that status lines appear progressively, not all at once after a delay.
